sentiment.label: risk_off
sentiment.reason_it: Il sentiment di mercato è risk-off a causa delle tensioni geopolitiche tra USA e Iran. Il VIX è sopra 20, indicando un mercato difensivo. Il DXY è forte, il che è bearish per le attività rischiose. Il prezzo del petrolio è in aumento, il che potrebbe portare a una recessione e a una maggiore inflazione.
sentiment.reason_en: The market sentiment is risk-off due to the geopolitical tensions between the US and Iran. The VIX is above 20, indicating a defensive market. The DXY is strong, which is bearish for risky assets. The oil price is increasing, which could lead to a recession and higher inflation.

market_impact_summary.it: Il mercato azionario è in calo a causa delle tensioni tra USA e Iran. L'S&P 500 è sceso dell'1,15%, mentre il VIX è salito al 26,15%. Il prezzo del petrolio è aumentato del 3,64%, raggiungendo $103,58 al barile. Il DXY è forte, +0,40% a 99,34. Il TLT è stabile, -0,01% a 86,38. L'oro è sceso dello 0,77% a $4370,10/oz.
market_impact_summary.en: The stock market is down due to tensions between the US and Iran. The S&P 500 is down 1.15%, while the VIX is up to 26.15%. The oil price is up 3.64% to $103.58 per barrel. The DXY is strong, +0.40% to 99.34. The TLT is stable, -0.01% to 86.38. Gold is down 0.77% to $4370.10/oz.

audio_script_it:
Benvenuti al vostro Morning Briefing quotidiano. Buongiorno e benvenuti all'aggiornamento finanziario di oggi.

La seduta asiatica ci consegna un Nikkei in aumento dello 0.93% a 51,992 punti e lo Shanghai in crescita dello 0.72% a 3840.58 punti. Questi dati positivi potrebbero anticipare un'apertura cauta delle borse europee, con un sentiment che sembra orientato verso una maggiore prudenza.

Guardando ai mercati occidentali, l'S&P 500 ieri ha chiuso con un guadagno dell'1.15% a 6581 punti, mentre il VIX è sceso del 2.35% a 26.15, segnalando una riduzione della paura tra gli investitori. Il Dollar Index (DXY) è salito dello 0.40% a 99.34, e il rendimento del decennale USA è sceso dell'1.30% al 4.33%. L'euro/dollaro è salito dello 0.24% a 1.15. Sul fronte obbligazionario, il TLT Bond USA 20Y è sceso dello 0.01% a 86.38.

Sul fronte geopolitico, le notizie sono concentrate sulle possibili trattative tra Usa e Iran, con un intervento del presidente Trump che ha fatto aumentare le scommesse sull'olio, con 580 milioni di dollari in poste prima del suo messaggio. La situazione in Iran resta tesa e potrebbe influenzare i mercati energetici.

In campo macroeconomico, gli ultimi dati disponibili non sono recentissimi, ma segnalano una crescita del PIL dell'Eurozona dello 0.2% nel quarto trimestre del 2025. La BCE ha lasciato invariato il tasso di interesse al 2.00% nella sua ultima riunione. Negli Usa, il prossimo dato importante sarà il PIL del primo trimestre, in uscita il 29 aprile. La Federal Reserve manterrà una posizione cauta se l'instabilità energetica dovesse protrarsi.

Nel comparto digitale, il Bitcoin è salito del 3.62% a $70,304 e l'Ethereum del 3.15% a $2,133. L'indice di Fear & Greed è a 11, in territorio di Extreme Fear.

Per oggi, l'attenzione sarà rivolta ai dati macroeconomici in uscita e all'evoluzione della situazione geopolitica. Gli investitori saranno prudenti in attesa di ulteriori sviluppi. Il quadro tecnico suggerisce una possibile continuazione della crescita, ma con una maggiore volatilità.

Passiamo ora al comparto degli asset digitali, con un'analisi approfondita delle criptovalute.

Iniziamo con il Bitcoin. Dal punto di vista tecnico, il prezzo attuale si attesta a $70,258, con un incremento del 2.04%. Tuttavia, i flussi degli ETF Bitcoin mostrano un deflusso di $-25.9M, secondo i dati di mercato. Questo indica una certa pressione di vendita sul mercato.

Passando alle altcoin, Ethereum guadagna il 3.15% e si posiziona a $2,133. Solana mostra un buon incremento del 3.52% a $90.11. Binance Coin, invece, registra una lieve flessione del 0.39% a $629.11. Questi movimenti indicano una certa vivacità nel mercato delle altcoin.

L'Indice di Paura ed Avidità si trova a 11, in territorio di Estrema Paura. Questo livello suggerisce che gli investitori sono molto cauti e potrebbe essere un segnale di un possibile punto di svolta nel mercato. La correlazione con i dati macroeconomici recenti, come il tasso di disoccupazione e l'inflazione, potrebbe influenzare ulteriormente il sentiment del mercato.

In sintesi, il mercato delle criptovalute mostra segnali contrastanti. Il Bitcoin registra un incremento di prezzo ma con deflussi dagli ETF, mentre le altcoin mostrano movimenti positivi. L'Indice di Paura ed Avidità è in territorio di Estrema Paura, suggerendo una possibile opportunità di acquisto. Tuttavia, è importante tenere d'occhio i dati macroeconomici e le notizie del settore per comprendere meglio le dinamiche del mercato.

Grazie per l'attenzione e a domani
audio_script_en:
Welcome to your daily morning market briefing. Good morning and welcome to today's financial update.

Global sentiment is cautious, with investors monitoring geopolitical developments. Overnight, US equities rebounded with the S&P 500 up 1.15% to 6581.00. Meanwhile, the VIX index eased 2.35% to 26.15, suggesting some reduction in volatility. Key data points include the US 10-year yield at 4.33%, down 1.30%, and the Dollar Index at 99.34, up 0.40%.

In traditional markets, equities are showing a mixed picture. The S&P 500 is up 1.15% to 6581.00, while the STOXX 600 is up 0.78% to 57.14. The Nikkei and Hang Seng indices in Asia saw gains of 0.93% and 1.61%, respectively. Currency markets are relatively stable, with the EUR/USD pair up 0.24% to 1.15. Commodities are seeing some movement, with Brent crude up 3.64% to $103.58 and gold down 0.77% to $4370.10 per ounce. Bond markets are seeing some buying, with the TLT Bond USA 20Y at 86.38, down 0.01%, and the US 10-year yield at 4.33%, down 1.30%.

Geopolitics remains a key focus, with ongoing tensions in the Middle East and Ukraine. The recent developments have led to increased uncertainty, with traders positioning ahead of potential outcomes. The Federal Reserve's stance on interest rates is being closely watched, with expectations that they will maintain their current position if the energy shock persists. Inflation concerns are also being monitored, with the PCE Core YoY at 3.1%. The global M2 liquidity continues to expand, up 4.3% YoY to $22.4T.

Moving on to the digital assets space...

Let's start with a deep dive into Bitcoin. The daily net inflow into BTC ETFs was $-25.9M, indicating a slight outflow from these investment vehicles. From a technical standpoint, BTC is trading at $70,258, up 2.04% on the day. The recent price swings have been quite volatile, with the Iran ceasefire drama rocking crypto markets. 

Let's pivot to the cryptocurrency markets and take a look at some of the major altcoins. Ethereum is trading at $2,133, up 3.15% on the day, while Solana is at $90.11, up 3.52%. Binance Coin is slightly down at $629.11, with a 0.39% loss. These moves suggest a relatively positive sentiment in the altcoin space.

In terms of market sentiment, the Fear & Greed Index is currently at 11, indicating extreme fear. This is often a contrarian indicator, suggesting that the market may be oversold. The correlation with traditional markets remains high, with the recent macro data from the US and Eurozone influencing crypto prices. The PCE Core YoY and Non-Farm Payrolls data, although old, still have an impact on market expectations. With the Fed's cautious stance, as noted by CoinShares, crypto ETF inflows have slowed to $230M.

Overall, it's a mixed bag in the digital assets space, with Bitcoin and altcoins showing varying degrees of strength. As we head into the next few weeks, all eyes will be on the upcoming macro data releases and their potential impact on the crypto market.

Thanks for tuning in. We'll be back tomorrow with another market update. Until then, stay informed and stay ahead. A quick look at what's on the radar for tomorrow and we'll wrap up here. Have a great day and we'll catch you soon.

articles.title: Trump’s Visit to China Won’t Fix Much, If It Even Happens
articles.snippet: President Donald Trump’s suggestion that he would likely delay his planned state visit to Beijing by “a month or so” to focus on the war with Iran underscores the fragility of the U.S.-Chinese truce that has held since October. Expectations of a U.S.-Chinese thaw had resurfaced in recent months as both sides worked toward a summit, reduced tariffs and carried out a series of good-faith measures like delaying implementation of export controls. But Trump’s March 15 call for China to help reopen the Strait of Hormuz before his state visit illustrated how quickly external events can reshape the bilateral agenda.Both The post Trump’s Visit to China Won’t Fix Much, If It Even Happens appeared first on War on the Rocks .

articles.title: Russia Is the Biggest Winner of the Iran War
articles.snippet: While the reasons US President Donald Trump has given for the war against the Islamic Republic do not hold up, it is already clear that Russian President Vladimir Putin stands to gain the most from it. With oil prices surging, Russia is poised to reap an enormous windfall that will help sustain its own war against Ukraine.

articles.title: The realities of economic security
articles.snippet: The Peterson Institute for International Economics presents a high-level analytical discussion of the realities of economic security, examining real-world trade-offs and vulnerabilities in today's geopolitically fractured world. Anjali Bhatt, host of PIIE Insider Live, moderates a candid discussion with PIIE experts: Warwick McKibbin, who presents global macroeconomic scenarios of the impact of the Middle East conflict; Cullen Hendrix, who discusses vulnerabilities of the global economy to the disrupted supply of commodities including food, fuel, and industrial inputs; and Jacob Funk…

articles.title: The Battlefield is the Next Betting Market
articles.snippet: When the United States and Israel struck Iran’s nuclear facilities in June last year, the operation caught many observers off guard — the planning was tightly concealed. By contrast, when Operation Epic Fury started in the early hours of Feb. 28, much of the world was staying up refreshing their screens, waiting for it to begin. Open source analysts tracked the usual indicators of escalation: satellite imagery, repositioning of carrier strike groups, and cryptic statements from officials. Intelligence agencies monitored missile deployments, while journalists quoted inside sources. Energy markets reacted to every rumor. Amid this flood of signals, one stood out: The post The Battlefield is the Next Betting Market appeared first on War on the Rocks .

articles.title: China’s Sinopec not Buying Iranian Crude despite U.S. Waiver
articles.snippet: Chinese state oil giant Sinopec, Asia’s biggest refiner by capacity, will not buy Iranian oil even after the U.S. waiver on purchases of crude from Iran loaded on tankers as of March 20, a senior Sinopec executive said on Monday. Sinopec, officially known as China Petroleum & Chemical Corporation, is weighing the potential risks of Iranian oil trade and “basically won't buy” Iranian crude, the company’s president Zhao Dong said, as carried by Reuters. The U.S. Treasury Department’s…

articles.title: Europe’s Gasoline Exports Shift to Asia as War Roils Fuel Markets
articles.snippet: Europe’s gasoline exports have started to increasingly shift to Asia in recent weeks as the crude supply shock in the Middle East is tightening Asian fuel supply. Europe usually ships most of its exported gasoline to the United States, West Africa, and South America. But in these unusual times of the biggest disruption in the oil market ever, more European gasoline cargoes are bound for Asia. Over the past week, at least 1.6 million barrels of gasoline, carried by three tankers, have loaded from Europe and are en route to…

articles.title: Chinese Publication Claims U.S. Has Two Months of Rare Earths Left
articles.snippet: The U.S. has already launched hundreds of missiles and precision-guided weapons in the escalating conflict with Iran, an air campaign that has consumed billions of dollars in advanced military hardware in just weeks. But a new warning circulating in Chinese and Western media suggests the materials needed to keep producing those weapons may be running dangerously low. Reports from the South China Morning Post and Reuters indicate Washington could have only weeks or months of certain rare-earth inventories available for defense manufacturing if supply…

articles.title: LNG Exports Plunge to 6-Month Low as War Throttles Supply
articles.snippet: Worldwide LNG exports have plunged in the past week to a six-month low as Middle Eastern supply collapsed with the de facto closed Strait of Hormuz and the outage at the world’s biggest LNG complex in Qatar. Global LNG shipments have plunged to about 1.1 million tons over the 10-day moving average, down by 20% since the war began, a Bloomberg analysis of Kpler vessel-tracking data showed. Qatar is the primary driver of the lost LNG supply following the halt of production and exports. Supply is also lost from the United Arab Emirates…

articles.title: Bitcoin Swings Wildly as Iran Ceasefire Drama Rocks Crypto Markets
articles.snippet: Bitcoin Magazine Bitcoin Swings Wildly as Iran Ceasefire Drama Rocks Crypto Markets Bitcoin kicked off the week with a surge above $71,000 before retracing, reflecting renewed market sensitivity to geopolitical developments between the U.S. and Iran. This post Bitcoin Swings Wildly as Iran Ceasefire Drama Rocks Crypto Markets first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman .

articles.summary_it: Bitcoin Magazine Bitcoin Swings Wildly as Iran Ceasefire Drama Rocks Crypto Markets Bitcoin kicked off the week with a surge above $71,000 before retracing, reflecting renewed market sensitivity to geopolitical developments between the U.S. and Iran. articles.summary_en: Bitcoin Magazine Bitcoin Swings Wildly as Iran Ceasefire Drama Rocks Crypto Markets Bitcoin kicked off the week with a surge above $71,000 before retracing, reflecting renewed market sensitivity to geopolitical developments between the U.S. and Iran. This post Bitcoin Swings Wildly as Iran Ceasefire Drama Rocks Crypto Markets first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman .

articles.title: Senator Warren questions whether MrBeast will market crypto to kids
articles.snippet: A letter to the influencer and the CEO of his holding company expressed concerns about whether the two will allow minors to trade crypto through a recently acquired banking app.
articles.title_it: Senator Warren questions whether MrBeast will market crypto to kids
articles.title_en: Senator Warren questions whether MrBeast will market crypto to kids

articles.title: Crypto ETF inflows slow to $230M as Fed caution dents momentum: CoinShares
articles.snippet: Weekly crypto ETP inflows reached $230 million despite $405 million in post-FOMC outflows, as Bitcoin led gains and Ether ended a three-week inflow streak, CoinShares reported.
articles.title_it: Crypto ETF inflows slow to $230M as Fed caution dents momentum: CoinShares
articles.title_en: Crypto ETF inflows slow to $230M as Fed caution dents momentum: CoinShares
